source_file: MEDIA STATEMENT NEW FMD CONTROL MEASURES GIVE FARMERS A CLEARER PATH TO RECOVERY WHILE PROTECTING TRADE.pdf
notes: Minister Steenhuisen approved new national FMD control measures 25 Jun 2026. Replaces Section 9 directives incl 2019 FMD Contingency Plan. Pending gazette publication. Key provisions: 16-day pathway to FMD abattoirs post clinical clearance; 42-day pathway to export-approved abattoirs; risk-based movement controls; provisions for communal/peri-urban systems; well-fenced farms may manage within affected portions only. Formal 12-month review planned.

NC State Veterinary Services FMD outbreak dashboard, dated 26 June 2026. 22 total reported outbreaks (up from 20 at 16 June); KPI card states 8 municipalities affected, but the municipality bar chart lists 9: Siyancuma 6, !Kheis 4, Joe Morolong 3, Thembelihle 3, Magareng 2, Ga-Segonyana 1, Gamagara 1, Dikgatlong 1, Dawid Kruiper 1 (sums to 22 outbreaks across 9 named municipalities, one more than the KPI card states; flagged, not reconciled). 4 State Veterinary Areas affected: Kuruman, Kimberley, De Aar, Upington -- Upington accounts for 50.0% (11 outbreaks) per chart footnote; remaining 11 split across the other three areas (approx. 3, 3, 5 by pie percentage, exact area-to-count mapping not fully legible). Diagnostic status: 10 (45.5%) laboratory confirmed, 12 (54.5%) clinical diagnosis. By production type: commercial farm 12 (54.5%), communal 4 (18.2%), feedlot 3 (13.6%), other 2 (9.1%), emerging farm 1 (4.5%). Two duplicate copies of an outbreak location map (same file content) also received; no additional numeric data beyond this dashboard.

UNOFFICIAL: received as PDMAF meeting screenshots (Teams recording captures) forwarded by KZN; the corresponding official JOC documents have not yet been circulated. Treat as provisional pending formal JOC confirmation. Provincial JOC-style headline figure: 45.2% coverage of the estimated 2.4-2.5 million provincial cattle population as at 30 May 2026 (target 80% / 1,920,000 for herd immunity). Matches the district-table sum of cattle (1,065,099) + pigs (20,396) = 1,085,495 exactly. Note: the same district table's own 'No of Animals Vaccinated' column total is 1,089,316 -- a difference of 3,821, which equals the eThekwini row value exactly; not reconciled, flagged for JOC confirmation. This figure is close to (and bridges) the previously flagged DoA figure of 1,163,193 as at 4 June 2026 (see animals_vaccinated_doa row), consistent with continued rollout over the intervening 5 days. Supersedes the stale 648,609 AgriSA-NAT carry-forward at 2026-05-21 per the 'trust latest provincial JOC' convention.
